bead.title: docs: state close-on-lossless-decomposition lifecycle
bead.description:
PROBLEM
DDx decomposition docs still describe a decomposed parent as an open execution-ineligible container. That leaves stale parent beads in the ready queue model and conflicts with the intended operational rule: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition completes the decomposed bead, while generated children remain executable work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children, and docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/beads.md:43 still treats `execution-eligible=false` decomposed containers as an ordinary current queue state.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:190 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:233 describe the decomposer leaving the oversized bead open/execution-ineligible.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md contain matching decomposition lifecycle language that must stay consistent with TD-031.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and FEAT-010 so they state the same rule: a lossless child, sibling, or replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition.
- State that historical open `execution-eligible=false` decomposed containers are legacy/backfill cases only, not the normative path for new decomposition.
- Keep legacy dead-intermediate and historical cleanup wording intact where it describes already-existing open containers.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change agent prompt code, generated skill references, lifecycle implementation, or tests; those are separate child beads under ddx-e9542e0d.
- Do not drain or close existing historical decomposed queue entries.
- Do not implement parent/ancestor dependency doctor repair from ddx-130cf9ab.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e9542e0d.
Deps: No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `docs/triage/decomposition.md`, `docs/beads.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md`, and matching `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` decomposition text state that a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition.
2. The same docs state that historical open `execution-eligible=false` decomposed containers are legacy/backfill only, not the normative path for new decomposition.
3. No updated normative decomposition passage says new lossless decomposition should keep the parent open with child dependency edges.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TooLargeDecomposed|DecomposeDoesNotSelfDep|PostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges|PostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose|AutoDecomposeEpic|parent_ancestor_in_deps' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
